description:
`/bw delegate` currently launches a tmux-backed worker, but the parent session has no dependable completion acknowledgment or automatic follow-up path. In practice this leaves work sitting in the delegated worktree unless a human later remembers to inspect `/bw workers`, and it makes the real operating model feel half-baked.

This ticket should make delegated execution feel remotely real-world usable. Scope:
- define and implement the expected end-to-end lifecycle after `/bw delegate`
- ensure completed workers reach a clear terminal state without relying on vague manual interpretation
- make parent/operator feedback explicit when a delegated ticket is done, still running, blocked, or exited without landing cleanly
- ensure there is a known follow-up path so delegated work does not just sit in a worktree with no acknowledgment
- tighten the relationship between worker exit, landing verification, cleanup policy, and operator-visible status
- add tests/docs for the resulting behavior if they are intrinsic to the implementation

Goal: after this ticket, we should be able to judge whether delegated worker flow is genuinely functional for real usage or still too far from the intended vision.
title: Make delegated worker completion actually usable for real workflows
